title: Frontend WebSocket Frame Envelope
description: Common envelope carried by every frontend WebSocket fan-out frame (RFC-007 §3). Frame schemas compose this via allOf, add their `type` discriminator and message-specific fields, then close the object with `unevaluatedProperties: false`. This is the same identity/timestamp/version envelope the MQTT contract carries (contracts/mqtt/envelope.schema.json), one transport down — frontend-ws is a local copy rather than a cross-contract $ref, matching how frontend-rest keeps its own common definitions. additionalProperties/unevaluatedProperties are intentionally left unset here so this schema annotates only its own four fields — setting additionalProperties: true would mark every property as evaluated and defeat the composing frame's unevaluatedProperties: false.

schema_version.description: Major version of the frame schema. Additive, backward-compatible changes do not bump it; breaking changes do (RFC-007 §5). Integer per RFC-007 — the data-model §2 sketch's string is illustrative.

greenhouse_id.description: Stable lowercase kebab slug, unique site-wide (e.g. gh-a, lettuce-north). Same identity used in MQTT topics, REST paths, and DB rows — no translation layer.

zone_id.description: Lowercase kebab slug, unique within a greenhouse (e.g. bench-a). Present for zone-scoped telemetry frames; null for greenhouse-scoped frames (status, drift, and event are always greenhouse-scoped).

ts.description: Frame timestamp, originating from the controller's injected clock. RFC 3339 / ISO 8601, UTC, millisecond precision (e.g. 2026-06-17T14:03:00.000Z). For a telemetry frame this is the sample instant the readings share. On a real-hardware / 1× run it tracks wall-clock; under an accelerated simulated run it is the simulated instant, advancing at the active time-scale (controller HAL §7) — so the SPA plots telemetry on simulated time directly.
